news package¶

Submodules¶

news.admin module¶

class news.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)¶

Bases: UserAdmin

fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Role and subscriptions', {'fields': ('role', 'publisher_subscriptions', 'journalist_subscriptions')}))¶
property media¶

news.api_views module¶

REST API views for the News Application.

class news.api_views.ApprovedArticleLogAPI(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.AllowAny'>]¶
post(request)¶
class news.api_views.ArticleDetailAPI(**kwargs)¶

Bases: RetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView

get_queryset()¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

perform_destroy(instance)¶
perform_update(serializer)¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news.api_views.ArticleListCreateAPI(**kwargs)¶

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

get_queryset()¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

perform_create(serializer)¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news.api_views.SubscribedArticlesAPI(**kwargs)¶

Bases: ListAPIView

get_queryset()¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

ready()¶

news.forms module¶

Forms used throughout the News Application.

news.models module¶

Database models for the News Application.

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: Model

Represents a news article written by a journalist.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

approved¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id¶
content¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

newsletters¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶
publisher¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

publisher_id¶
title¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class news.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model supporting Reader, Journalist and Editor roles.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

EDITOR = 'editor'¶
JOURNALIST = 'journalist'¶
exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

READER = 'reader'¶
ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('journalist', 'Journalist'), ('editor', 'Editor')]¶
articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

auth_token¶

Accessor to the related object on the reverse side of a one-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Restaurant(Model):
    place = OneToOneField(Place, related_name='restaurant')

Place.restaurant is a ReverseOneToOneDescriptor instance.

date_joined¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

editor_publishers¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

role¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

save(*args, **kwargs)¶

Clear role-specific fields when the user role changes.

Readers can have subscriptions. Journalists should not use reader subscription fields. Editors should not use reader subscription fields.

user_permissions¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class news.permissions.IsEditor¶

Bases: BasePermission

has_permission(request, view)¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

Bases: BasePermission

has_permission(request, view)¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news.permissions.IsReader¶

Bases: BasePermission

has_permission(request, view)¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

test_reader_cannot_delete_article()¶

news.urls module¶

news.views module¶

Views for the News Application.

This module contains all page views for authentication, articles, newsletters, publishers, subscriptions and editor approval.

news.views.approve_article(request, article_id)¶

Approve an article, notify subscribers and send the approved article to the API endpoint.

news.views.approve_articles(request)¶

Allow a journalist to create a new article. New articles require editor approval before publication.

news.views.article_detail(request, article_id)¶
news.views.article_list(request)¶
news.views.create_article(request)¶

Allow a journalist to create a new article. New articles require editor approval before publication.

news.views.create_newsletter(request)¶
news.views.create_publisher(request)¶
news.views.dashboard(request)¶
news.views.delete_article(request, article_id)¶
news.views.delete_newsletter(request, newsletter_id)¶
news.views.edit_article(request, article_id)¶
news.views.edit_newsletter(request, newsletter_id)¶
news.views.home(request)¶
news.views.manage_subscriptions(request)¶
news.views.my_articles(request)¶
news.views.newsletter_detail(request, newsletter_id)¶
news.views.newsletter_list(request)¶
news.views.notify_subscribers(article, request)¶
news.views.publisher_detail(request, publisher_id)¶
news.views.publisher_list(request)¶
news.views.register(request)¶
news.views.toggle_journalist_subscription(request, journalist_id)¶
news.views.toggle_publisher_subscription(request, publisher_id)¶
